options.text: A JSON object parsed from the response body
The raw bytes of the response body as a string immediately
A Promise that resolves with the response body as a string
The HTTP status code as a text string

questions.explanationMd: `response.text()` returns a Promise that resolves with a string representation of the response body.

questions.questionMd: When loading album data from a custom plain-text file, which of the following is a disadvantage mentioned in the lecture?

options.text: Numbers must be manually converted from strings using functions like parseInt()
The file cannot be loaded using the fetch() API
Plain-text files cannot be served over HTTP
Text files do not support arrays of items

questions.questionMd: What does `response.status` contain when used inside a fetch success handler?

options.text: The HTTP status code of the request, such as 200
The text content of the fetched resource
A boolean indicating whether the request succeeded
The name of the file that was fetched

questions.questionMd: What does `JSON.parse()` accept as its argument?

options.text: A JavaScript object to be serialized
A Promise returned by fetch()
A fetch response object
A JSON-formatted string

questions.questionMd: Compared to a custom plain-text format, what is a primary advantage of storing data in JSON format when using `fetch()`?

options.text: JavaScript has built-in support to automatically convert a JSON string into an object, eliminating manual parsing
JSON files are always smaller in size than plain-text files
JSON is the only format supported by the fetch() API
JSON files do not require a server to be accessed

questions.questionMd: Which of the following correctly rewrites `fetch('f.txt').then(onResponse)` using an intermediate variable?

options.text: let p = onResponse; p.fetch('f.txt');
const p = fetch('f.txt'); p.resolve(onResponse);
const p = new Promise('f.txt'); p.then(onResponse);
const p = fetch('f.txt'); p.then(onResponse);

questions.questionMd: In REST API terminology, what is the "base URL"?

options.text: The fallback URL used when the primary endpoint is unavailable
The root address of the API server to which all endpoint paths are appended
The full URL including all query parameters for a specific request
The URL of the web page that consumes the API

questions.questionMd: How do LANs and WANs relate to the concepts of Intranet and Internet respectively?

options.text: LANs form private internal networks (Intranet), while WANs connect these networks together to form the Internet
LANs use wireless connections only, while WANs use wired connections only
LANs are always public networks, while WANs are always private networks
LANs operate at the application layer, while WANs operate at the physical layer
A Promise
The raw text content of the requested resource
An XMLHttpRequest object
A JSON-parsed JavaScript object

questions.questionMd: What is the fundamental difference between a URL that points to a static file and a URL that represents a REST API endpoint?

options.text: A REST API endpoint returns HTML, while a static file URL always returns JSON
A REST API endpoint causes the server to dynamically generate a response, whereas a static file URL causes the server to return a stored file
A static file URL must include query parameters, while a REST API endpoint never does
A REST API endpoint always requires POST, while static file URLs always use GET

questions.questionMd: Which statement correctly distinguishes the Internet from an Intranet?

options.text: The Internet uses IPv6 only, while an Intranet uses IPv4 only
The Internet is limited to universities, while an Intranet is open to the public
The Internet uses UDP, while an Intranet uses TCP exclusively
The Internet is the global interconnected network, while an Intranet is a private network for a limited group of users

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, what does it mean to "deserialize" a string?

options.text: To split a string into an array using a separator
To turn a serialized string back into a JavaScript object
To encode a string using HTML entities
To convert a JavaScript object into a JSON string

questions.questionMd: A Promise object can be in one of three states. Which of the following is NOT one of those states?

options.text: Fulfilled
Resolved
Pending
Rejected

questions.explanationMd: The correct states are Pending, Fulfilled, and Rejected. 'Resolved' is commonly used in conversation, but the official state name is Fulfilled.

questions.questionMd: When a method is used as a DOM event handler without binding, what does `this` refer to inside that method?

options.text: The global window object
The class instance that owns the method
The HTML element that the event handler was attached to
The event object passed to the handler

questions.questionMd: What is the key difference between `navigator.getUserMedia` and `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia`?

options.text: The deprecated version uses Promises; the newer version uses callbacks
Only the deprecated version supports video streaming
The newer version requires HTTPS while the deprecated version does not
The newer version returns a Promise, the deprecated version uses callbacks
